title: Design reviewed worktree cleanup apply path
goal: Define and implement a guarded cleanup path for worktree-hygiene candidates without deleting worktrees from a report alone.

metadata.source: Registered after PR #253 intentionally shipped worktree-hygiene as read-only only.
metadata.safety_boundary: Cleanup must be reviewed, path-scoped, branch-aware, dirty-state-protected, process-aware and reversible where possible. The external worktree-admin gate and current exact-path lease check remain mandatory operator preconditions.
metadata.outcome: A reviewed, candidate-explicit worktree cleanup apply path is merged. It preserves the read-only report default, requires a hash-bound reviewed plan, refuses canonical, dirty, missing, locked, overlapping, unmerged and procfs-observably process-used worktrees, removes without force, never deletes branches, emits a fresh post-clean report and attempts best-effort rollback after partial failure.
validation: Exact implementation head 0d5b57a collected and passed 491 of 491 repository tests.
Post-merge checkout at 2c12ed0 passed the same 491 of 491 repository tests.
Thirty-two focused worktree-hygiene, approval and runtime-observation tests passed before and after merge.
Ruff passed for all changed Python and test files.
Bureau check reported valid=true before and after merge.
Registry Truth was healthy with zero errors and no BUR-2026-005-T016 finding before merge.
GitHub validate (3.10) and validate (3.12) succeeded at the reviewed head.
No existing live worktree was cleaned by this task; effect behavior was proven only in isolated temporary Git repositories.

verified_acceptance.explicit-candidates: Tests prove plan creation accepts only explicit absolute linked-worktree paths, rejects relative, duplicate and overlapping candidates, and never expands report findings into effects.
verified_acceptance.review-binding: The reviewed plan is bound to repository identity, exact candidate identity and unchanged plan bytes; missing, mismatched or stale bindings fail before effect.
verified_acceptance.dirty-refusal: Focused tests prove refusal for canonical, dirty, missing, locked, unmerged and procfs-observably process-used worktrees. No force option exists.
verified_acceptance.branch-preservation: A real temporary-repository apply removes only the reviewed linked worktree while preserving its local branch and an unrelated worktree; implementation contains no branch deletion command.
verified_acceptance.rollback-boundary: A simulated later-candidate failure proves the already removed branch-backed worktree is restored; documentation and result schema keep rollback explicitly best-effort.
verified_acceptance.post-clean-report: The real temporary-repository apply returns a fresh hygiene report proving the reviewed path absent and the unrelated worktree present.
verified_acceptance.coordination-boundary: Code and documentation preserve external worktree-admin gate and exact-path lease checks as operator preconditions and explicitly disclaim authority over Grabowski lease truth and process state beyond procfs visibility.

acceptance.assertion: Apply mode accepts only explicit absolute candidate paths from a reviewed plan, not all hygiene findings.
Review is bound to repository identity and exact candidate path, head, branch and safety-state hashes; stale or modified plans fail closed before an effect.
Canonical, dirty, missing, locked, procfs-observably process-used or not-fully-merged worktrees are refused; no force mode exists.
Cleanup calls git worktree remove without force and never deletes local or remote branches.
If a later candidate or post-clean gate fails, already removed worktrees are restored where Git still permits exact reconstruction from the reviewed branch or detached head.
After cleanup, worktree-hygiene emits a fresh post-clean report proving the reviewed paths are absent while unrelated worktrees remain.
Documentation requires the short Bureau worktree-admin gate and a separate current check for foreign exact path leases; the hygiene report never claims authority over Grabowski lease truth.
